policy.offline: Regenerate on PRs that touch load path, COPY, ledger/reuse, bench, or compare harness (`mise run compare-scoreboard`). CI runs `--check`.
policy.cloud: Competitor AI / warehouse legs are env-gated and budget-capped; not required for PR green.

scenarios.title: Parquet → SQL → PostgreSQL load path
scenarios.notes: Wall time on like-for-like PostgreSQL load is server-dominated; CPU is the clearer differentiator. Machine: Apple M3 laptop — relative evidence, not a cloud SLA.

rows.detail: wall 6.9–9.2 s; CPU 1.2–1.4 s; ~0.5 GiB RSS (3 runs)
wall tie with Pramen; CPU 6.9–10.9 s; ~45 MiB RSS
engine ceiling for the same SQL; exposes load-path cost
not a PostgreSQL load — cheapest possible destination
42.8 s wall — Pramen 3.1× faster on this fixture

scenarios.title: Semantic result reuse vs naive re-dispatch
scenarios.notes: Offline mock provider + SQLite ledger. Live Bedrock confirmation deferred to S2.2.

rows.detail: vs per-row dispatch with no ledger

scenarios.title: Governed enrichment vs Redpanda Connect AI processor
scenarios.notes: Config scaffold checked in. Run with COMPARE_REDPANDA=1 and provider credentials; publish a dated report under docs/benchmarks/ before flipping status to measured.

scenarios.title: Structured extraction vs DocETL
scenarios.notes: Pipeline scaffold checked in. Run with COMPARE_DOCETL=1; publish dated report before measured.

scenarios.title: Operational PG enrich vs in-warehouse AI SQL
scenarios.notes: Qualitative only until a budgeted L3 harness exists (Databricks ai_query / Snowflake Cortex / BigQuery AI.GENERATE_TABLE). See orientation for when the warehouse wins.
